The Cosmetic Products Regulation (EC) No 1223/2009 (Restriction of Chemical Substances) (Amendment and Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2026
items.description: Following advice from the Scientific Advisory Group on Chemical Safety of Non-Food and Non-Medicinal Consumer Products that there is a potential risk to human health from the use of certain chemical substances in cosmetic products, these Regulations amend Regulation (EC) No 1223/2009 (“the Cosmetic Regulation”). Specifically, regulation 2(2) amends Annex 2 to the Cosmetic Regulation (list of substances prohibited in cosmetic products) (“Annex 2”) to prohibit the use of 3-(4'-methylbenzylidene)-camphor in cosmetic products. Regulation 2(4) amends the Preamble to Annex 5 (list of preservatives allowed in cosmetic products) to the Cosmetic Regulation to reduce the threshold for warning labelling requirements for formaldehyde releasing preservatives used in cosmetic products. These amendments apply from 15th July 2026.

items.title: The Aviation Security (Amendment, Revocation and Consequential Provision) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations revoke six items of secondary assimilated law (as defined in section 21(1) of the Retained EU Law (Revocation and Reform) Act 2023 (c. 28)) which make provision in the field of aviation security and make amendments to other legislation.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Rates, Limits and Thresholds Amendments, National Insurance Funds Payments and Extension of Veteran’s Relief) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (c. 4) and corresponding provisions in the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (c. 7) (“the Acts”), the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 2001 (S.I. 2001/1004) (“the Contributions Regulations”) and the National Insurance Contributions Act 2022 (c. 9) (“the NICA 2022”). The amendments have effect from 6th April 2026.

items.title: The Child Benefit and Guardian’s Allowance Up-rating Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends the Child Benefit (Rates) Regulations 2006 (S.I. 2006/965); the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (c. 4); and the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (c. 7).

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages and Civil Partnerships (Fees) (Amendment and Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages and Civil Partnerships (Fees) Regulations 2016 (“the Fees Regulations”), which set fees for applications and services in relation to the registration of births, deaths, marriages and civil partnerships in England and Wales. Regulations 4 and 5 increase the amounts of fees payable in order to move towards cost recovery in line with government policy in relation to public resources. Regulation 6 makes transitional provisions so that the fees in force immediately before 6th April 2026 continue to apply where an application or request for a service is made before that date, or where the service arises from action taken before that date. Where a fee has already been paid at the amount in force before 6th April 2026, the amended fee does not apply.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Light Dues) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Light Dues) Regulations 2025 (S.I. 2025/278) (the “2025 Regulations”).

items.title: The On-demand Programme Services (Tier 1 Services) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations specify a description of on-demand programme services, or non-UK on-demand programme services, as Tier 1 services for the purposes of Chapter 3 of Part 4A of the Communications Act 2003 (c. 21) (on-demand programme services: regulation of Tier 1 services).

items.title: The Armed Forces Commissioner (Service Complaints Investigations) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Armed Forces Commissioner Act 2025 (c. 23) (“AFCA 25”) amended the Armed Forces Act 2006 (c. 52) (“AFA 06”) to introduce a new office of Armed Forces Commissioner. The Armed Forces Commissioner will also exercise the functions of the Service Complaints Ombudsman and the AFCA 25 accordingly abolishes this office.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Renewable Energy Projects) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Non-Domestic Rating (Renewable Energy Projects) Regulations 2013 (S.I. 2013/108) (“the 2013 Regulations”).

items.title: The Supreme Court Fees (Amendment) Order 2026
items.description: The Supreme Court Fees Order 2024 (S.I. 2024/148) (“the 2024 Order”) prescribes the fees payable in the Supreme Court. The fees set by the 2024 Order reflected historic inflation up to March 2023.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Sunderland) Regulations 2026
The Energy-Intensive Industry Electricity Support Payments and Levy (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Energy-Intensive Industry Electricity Support Payments and Levy Regulations 2024 (S.I. 2024/409) (“the 2024 Regulations”). The 2024 Regulations entitle eligible energy-intensive businesses to electricity support payments in respect of network charging costs and set out how such payments are to be funded (through a levy on electricity suppliers).

items.title: The Seafarers’ Wages (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 14(1) of the Seafarers’ Wages Regulations 2024 (the “2024 Regulations”) to increase the hourly rates specified for the purposes of the national minimum wage equivalent. These Regulations come into force on 1st April 2026.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Definition of Qualifying Retail, Hospitality or Leisure Hereditament) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the list of excluded purposes contained in Schedule 1 to the Non-Domestic Rating (Definition of Qualifying Retail, Hospitality or Leisure Hereditament) Regulations 2025 (S.I. 2025/1093) (“the 2025 Regulations”). The 2025 Regulations provide a definition for the term “qualifying retail, hospitality or leisure hereditament” for the purposes of determining whether a lower multiplier may be applied to calculate the business rates due in relation to the hereditament in question. Schedule 1 of the 2025 Regulations contains a list of excluded purposes and where a hereditament is wholly or mainly used for one or more of those purposes the hereditament is not a “qualifying retail, hospitality or leisure hereditament”. These Regulations remove a hereditament that is wholly or mainly used as a marina, wharf, pier or jetty from Schedule 1. This means that a hereditament wholly or mainly used as a marina, wharf, pier of jetty may be a qualifying retail, hospitality or leisure hereditament where the other requirements of the 2025 Regulations are met.

items.title: The Individual Savings Account (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Individual Savings Account Regulations 1998 by:

items.title: The Relief for Creative Industries (Additional Information Requirements and Miscellaneous Amendments) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Relief for Creative Industries (Additional Information Requirements and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (S.I. 2024/320) (“the 2024 Regulations”).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Hall Farm, Langford, Newark) (Emergency) (Revocation) Regulations 2026
The Child Trust Funds (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Child Trust Funds Regulations 2004 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Humber Bridge Act 1959 (Amendment) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends the Humber Bridge Act 1959 (“the Act”) using the power conferred by section 6(1)(e) of the Act, as substituted by section 3(3) of, and paragraph 1 of Schedule 1 to, the Humber Bridge Act 2013. The Act provides for the construction and ongoing maintenance of the Humber Bridge and approaching roads and establishes the Humber Bridge Board (“the Board”) to execute the implementation of the Act.

items.title: The Immigration and Nationality (Fees) (Amendment) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends the Immigration and Nationality (Fees) Order 2016 (S.I. 2016/770) (“the 2016 Order”).

items.title: The Customs (Tariff and Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 2) Regulations 2026
items.description: Regulation 2 amends the definition of “the authorised use document” in regulation 32(2) of the Customs (Special Procedures and Outward Processing) (EU Exit) Regulations 2018 (S.I. 2018/1249) to refer to a new version of that document. The new version of this document makes amendments to align with EU commodity code updates. It will also introduce authorised use measures to reduce import duty on goods that will be used to manufacture offshore wind turbines, including cables that link the wind farm to the mainland substations and auxiliary and low-voltage systems for incorporation in onshore substations and offshore substations used in offshore wind energy production.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Mountain Ash) (Emergency) (Revocation) Regulations 2026
The National Health Service (Primary Dental Services and Dental Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Contracts) Regulations 2005 (S.I. 2005/3361) (“the GDS Contracts Regulations”), the National Health Service (Personal Dental Services Agreements) Regulations 2005 (S.I. 2005/3373) (“the PDS Agreements Regulations”) and the National Health Service (Dental Charges) Regulations 2005 (S.I. 2005/3477) (“the NHS Charges Regulations”).

items.title: The National Savings (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Savings Regulations 2015 (S.I. 2015/623) (“the 2015 Regulations”).

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction Schemes (Prescribed Requirements) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: Section 13A of the Local Government Finance Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”) requires each billing authority in England to make a scheme specifying the reductions which are to apply to amounts of council tax payable by persons, or classes of person, whom the billing authority considers are in financial need. The Council Tax Reduction Schemes (Prescribed Requirements) (England) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) prescribe matters which must be included in such a scheme in addition to those matters which must be included in such a scheme by virtue of paragraph 2 of Schedule 1A to the 1992 Act.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Exmouth, Devon) (Emergency) Regulations 2026
The Power to Award Degrees etc. (Lamda Limited) (Amendment) Order 2026
items.description: This Order varies the Power to Award Degrees etc. (Lamda Limited) Order 2021 (as amended by S.I. 2024/1063), to remove the time restriction, so that Lamda Limited’s (UKPRN: 10003758) authorisation remains for an indefinite period.

items.title: The Employment Rights Act 2025 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitional and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations bring into force certain provisions of the Employment Rights Act 2025 (c. 36) (“the 2025 Act”). These are the first commencement Regulations made under the 2025 Act. Certain provisions were brought into force upon Royal Assent by virtue of section 159(1) of the 2025 Act, and two months after Royal Assent by virtue of section 159(2) of the 2025 Act.

items.title: The Stockport Town Centre Mayoral Development Corporation (Establishment) Order 2026
items.description: This Order establishes a Mayoral development corporation in relation to an area, designated as a Mayoral development area, which encompasses the area shown bounded externally by the inner edge of a red line on the map referred to in article 2 of this Order. Copies of the map may be inspected free of charge by prior appointment with the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government, Local Growth Delivery Unit, at 2 Marsham Street, London, SW1P 4DF and the Greater Manchester Combined Authority, Tootal Buildings, 56 Oxford Street, Manchester, M1 6EU.

items.title: The Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 5) Regulations 2026
items.description: These are the fifth commencement Regulations made under the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (c. 18) (“the Act”).

The Customs (Tariff and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2026
items.description: Regulation 2 amends the licensing table in Schedule 2 to the Customs (Tariff Quotas) (EU Exit) Regulations 2020 (S.I. 2020/1432) which identifies the tariff quotas that are administered by licence. It removes preferential United States beef quota 05.4010 from this table to reflect a change in the administration of this quota from ‘licensed’ to ‘first come first served’. This quota is in respect of the General Terms for the United States of America and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland Economic Prosperity Deal, concluded on 8th May 2025.

items.title: The School and Early Years Finance (England) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations make provision for local authorities’ financial arrangements in relation to the funding of maintained schools and providers of funded early years provision in England, for the financial year 2026-2027.

items.title: The Income Tax (Indexation of Blind Person’s Allowance and Married Couple’s Allowance) Order 2026
items.description: This Order fulfils the indexation requirements in section 57 (indexation of allowances) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (c. 3) in respect of blind person’s allowance and married couple’s allowance, which are increased by reference to the Consumer Price Index. This increase has effect for the tax year 2026-2027 (and subsequent tax years).

items.title: The Income Tax (Indexation of Qualifying Care Relief Amounts) Order 2026
items.description: This Order fulfils the indexation requirements in section 828A (indexation of the fixed amount and the amount per adult and child) of the Income Tax (Trading and Other Income) Act 2005 (c. 5) in respect of qualifying care relief amounts, which are increased by reference to the consumer price index. This increase has effect for the tax year 2026-27 and subsequent tax years.

items.title: The Local Government Finance Act 1988 (Calculation of Non-Domestic Rating Multipliers for Retail, Hospitality or Leisure) (England) Regulations 2026
items.description: Paragraphs A6A and 9B of Schedule 7 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (c. 41) (“the Act”) were inserted by the Non-Domestic Rating (Multipliers and Private Schools) Act 2025 (c. 12), enabling the Treasury to make regulations for additional non-domestic rating multipliers for the purpose of the calculation of business rates under the Act. These Regulations specify the additional multipliers that will apply to retail, hospitality or leisure (“RHL”) hereditaments in respect of financial years beginning on or after 1st April 2026. The Regulations specify the calculation for the RHL multipliers that apply to occupied RHL hereditaments, including those located in a special authority (as defined in section 144(6) of the Act).

items.title: The Treatment of Conformity Assessment Bodies (Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement between the Government of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of the Republic of India) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations are made in exercise of the powers conferred by sections 1 and 2 of the Product Regulation and Metrology Act 2025 in order to implement Article 7.7.9 (National Treatment - Conformity Assessment) of the Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement between the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Government of the Republic of India, signed in Buckinghamshire on 24 July 2025.

items.title: The Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exceptions) (Amendment) (England and Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exceptions) Order 1975 (“the 1975 Order”).

items.title: The Universal Credit (Transitional Provisions) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Universal Credit (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2014 (S.I. 2014/1230) (“Transitional Regulations”).

items.title: The Football Spectators (2026 FIFA World Cup Control Period) Order 2026
items.description: This Order prescribes the control period under the Football Spectators Act 1989 (c. 37) (“the 1989 Act”) for the 2026 FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Association) World Cup in the United States of America, Mexico and Canada. The control period begins on 1st June 2026, which is ten days before the first match in the tournament, and ends when the last match in the tournament is finished or cancelled. The last match is due to be played on 19th July 2026.

items.title: The Upper Ouse Water Management Board Order 2026
items.description: This Order confirms, with modifications, a Scheme submitted by the Environment Agency for the amalgamation of the Alconbury and Ellington Internal Drainage District, the Bedfordshire and River Ivel Internal Drainage District and the Buckingham and River Ouzel Internal Drainage District into one new internal drainage district (“the Upper Ouse Internal Drainage District”). The Alconbury and Ellington Internal Drainage Board, the Bedfordshire and River Ivel Internal Drainage Board and the Buckingham and River Ouzel Internal Drainage Board are abolished and a new internal drainage board (“the Upper Ouse Water Management Board”) is constituted for the new internal drainage district.

items.title: The Lyme Regis, Bridport (West Bay) and Weymouth Harbour Revision Order 2026

The effect of the designation is that these bodies are required to prepare and present to the Treasury such financial information in relation to that financial year as the Treasury require to enable them to prepare Whole of Government Accounts.

Those with heat network authorisations are referred to as “authorised persons”. The amendments provide for the following—

The Electricity and Gas (Standards of Performance) (Suppliers) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Electricity and Gas (Standards of Performance) (Suppliers) Regulations 2015 (the “Principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Regulated Activities) (Providing Targeted Support) (Amendment) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Regulated Activities) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/544) (“the Regulated Activities Order”). The Regulated Activities Order specifies kinds of activities and investments for the purposes of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (c. 8).

items.title: The Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 (Commencement No. 3) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations bring into force specified provisions of the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 (c. 45) (“the 2022 Act”). These are the third commencement regulations made under the 2022 Act.

items.title: The Diocese of Leicester (Educational Endowments) (Market Harborough Church of England Infant School) Order 2026
The Access to the Countryside (Coastal Margin) (South Hayling to East Head) Order 2026
items.description: This Order appoints the day on which the access preparation period ends in relation to land which is coastal margin as a result of the approval by the Secretary of State of proposals contained in reports relating to coastal access along the long-distance route between South Hayling and East Head.

items.title: The M6 Toll Motorway (M6 Junction 3a to M6 Junction 11a) and M42 Motorway (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2026
The United Kingdom Internal Market Act 2020 (Exclusions from Market Access Principles: Glue Traps) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 1 to the United Kingdom Internal Market Act 2020 (c. 27) (“the 2020 Act”) to insert a further exclusion from the market access principles set out in Part 1 of the 2020 Act in respect of glue traps. The effect of the amendment is that the market access principles will not apply to, or affect the operation of, legislation so far as it prohibits the sale of glue traps in any part of the United Kingdom. For the purposes of this instrument, “sale” has the same meaning as in section 15 of the 2020 Act.

items.title: The Public Lending Right Scheme 1982 (Commencement of Variation) Order 2026
items.description: This Order brings into force on 9th February 2026 a variation of the Public Lending Right Scheme 1982 (“the Scheme”) made by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Free Zone (Customs Site No. 1 Celtic) Designation Order 2026
items.description: This Order designates the Customs Site No. 1 Celtic as a special area for customs purposes (a “free zone”) and appoints Dyfed Steels Limited as the responsible authority for the free zone designated by this Order pursuant to the Treasury’s powers under section 100A of the Customs and Excise Management Act 1979 (“CEMA”).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Chargeable Amounts) (England) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the Act”) provides for non-domestic hereditaments to be revalued every three years. On a revaluation new non-domestic rating lists are compiled. The next compilation date is 1st April 2026.

items.title: The Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (Commencement No. 6 and Transitional and Saving Provisions) Regulations 2026
items.description: These are the sixth commencement regulations made under the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (c. 18) (“the 2025 Act”).

items.title: The Pension Protection Fund and Occupational Pension Schemes (Levy Ceiling) Order 2026
items.description: This Order specifies the earnings percentage used to calculate the levy ceiling (article 3) and the amount of the levy ceiling (article 4) for use in relation to the Pension Protection Fund in the financial year beginning on 1st April 2026.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Helicopter Flight) (No. 3) Regulations 2026
The Sham Marriage and Civil Partnership (Referral of Proposed Marriages and Civil Partnerships) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: Part 4 of the Immigration Act 2014 (c. 22) (“the 2014 Act”) established a scheme for the referral of proposed marriages and civil partnerships to the Secretary of State, who must decide whether to investigate the proposed marriage or civil partnership to determine whether it is a sham. All proposed marriages and civil partnerships where at least one party is not exempt must be referred to the Secretary of State. Exempt persons include (among others) those who are exempt from immigration control.

items.title: The Sentencing Act 2026 (Commencement No. 1) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations bring into force specified provisions of the Sentencing Act 2026 (“the 2026 Act”). These are the first commencement Regulations made under the 2026 Act.

items.title: The Immigration (Electronic Travel Authorisations and the Islands) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations make provision about the effects in the United Kingdom of the grant or refusal under the law of any of the Islands of an authorisation in electronic form to travel to that Island (“an Island ETA”). They additionally make provision about the effects in the United Kingdom of the variation or cancellation under the law of any of the Islands of a UK electronic travel authorisation (“an ETA”) and of the variation or cancellation of an Island ETA by the Secretary of State or an Immigration Officer.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Dedham) Regulations 2026
The Forestry Act 1967 (Consent to Renewable Electricity Development) (England) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations require the Forestry Commissioners to obtain the consent of the Secretary of State before they exercise the power in section 3A of the Forestry Act 1967 (c. 10) to enable the construction of renewable electricity development upon forestry land in England.

items.title: The Access to the Countryside (Coastal Margin) (Isle of Wight) (No. 2) Order 2026
items.description: This Order appoints the day on which the access preparation period ends in relation to land which is coastal margin as a result of the approval by the Secretary of State of proposal contained in a report relating to coastal access along the long-distance route on the Isle of Wight between Culver Down and Binnel Bay. Copies of the notice of approval are available at www.gov.ukand may also be obtained by writing to the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, Seacole Building, 2 Marsham Street, London SW1 4DF.

items.title: The Designation of Special Tax Sites (North East of Scotland Investment Zone) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations designate areas, known as a “special tax sites”, as special areas for the purposes of Parts 2 (plant and machinery allowances) and 2A (structures and buildings allowances) of the Capital Allowances Act 2001 (c. 2) (“CAA 2001”).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Rochdale, Greater Manchester) (Emergency) Regulations 2026
The Designation of Special Tax Sites (Glasgow City Region Investment Zone) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations designate areas, known as “special tax sites”, as special areas for the purposes of Parts 2 (plant and machinery allowances) and 2A (structures and buildings allowances) of the Capital Allowances Act 2001 (c. 2) (“CAA 2001”).

items.title: The Income Tax (Tax Treatment of Scottish Carer Supplement and Exemption of Carer’s Additional Person Payment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to Part 10 of the Income Tax (Earnings and Pensions) Act 2003 (c. 1) in consequence of two new social security benefits introduced by the Carer’s Assistance (Miscellaneous and Consequential Amendments, Revocation, Transitional and Saving Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2025 (S.S.I. 2025/340) from 15th March 2026. The amendments ensure the Scottish Carer Supplement is chargeable to income tax and exempt the Carer’s Additional Person Payment from income tax.

items.title: The Local Government (Exclusion of Non-commercial Considerations) (England) Order 2026
items.description: Section 17(5) of the Local Government Act 1988 (c. 9) (“the 1988 Act”) contains a list of matters (non-commercial matters) by reference to which specified authorities may not exercise the functions listed in section 17(4) of that Act, which include functions in relation to proposed public supply or works contracts with the authority.

items.title: The Immigration (Certificate of Entitlement to Right of Abode in the United Kingdom) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Immigration (Certificate of Entitlement to Right of Abode in the United Kingdom) Regulations 2006 (“the 2006 Regulations”) (S.I. 2006/3145) principally to make necessary changes to enable the issuing and revoking of digital certificates of entitlement.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Changes to Years of Ordinary Elections) (England) Order 2026
items.description: This Order changes the year of ordinary election of councillors for those English councils specified in the Schedule from 2026 to 2027. The years in which subsequent ordinary elections of councillors for those councils take place are not changed by this Order, except for Thurrock Council for which subsequent ordinary elections are, as a result of this Order, to be held in 2031 and every four years thereafter.

items.title: The Civil Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2026
items.description: These Rules amend the Civil Procedure Rules 1998 (S.I. 1998/3132) by—

items.title: The Immigration (Isle of Man) (Amendment) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends the Immigration (Isle of Man) Order 2008 (S.I. 2008/680) (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Amendment) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends the Air Navigation Order 2016 (S.I. 2016/765) (“ANO 2016”) and Schedule 9 to the Air Traffic Management and Unmanned Aircraft Act 2021 (c. 12).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Non-Domestic Rating (Demand Notices) (Wales) Regulations 2017 (“the 2017 Regulations”) provide for the contents of non-domestic rates demand notices which are served by or on behalf of billing authorities in Wales.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance and Accounting) (Wales) (Amendments relating to Minimum Revenue Provision) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Authorities (Capital Finance and Accounting) (Wales) Regulations 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Student Finance) (Amounts) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend—

items.title: The Adoption Support Services (Adoption Support Agencies) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations are made by the Welsh Ministers under powers conferred by sections 2(6)(b), 140(7), and 142(4) and (5)(a) of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 (“the 2002 Act”). They revoke and replace the Adoption Support Services (Wales) Regulations 2019 (“the 2019 Regulations”).

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction Schemes (Prescribed Requirements and Default Scheme) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Reduction Schemes and Prescribed Requirements (Wales) Regulations 2013 (“the Prescribed Requirements Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction Schemes (Default Scheme) (Wales) Regulations 2013 (“the Default Scheme Regulations”) made under section 13A(4) and (5) of, and Schedule 1B to, the Local Government Finance Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”).

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Wales) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2026
items.description: This Order is the first commencement order made under the Local Government Finance (Wales) Act 2024 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Provision of Information About Changes of Circumstances) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations impose a duty on a person (“P”) to provide information to billing authorities when P becomes the ratepayer of a hereditament or when certain changes occur in relation to the occupation of a hereditament for which P is the ratepayer (the “notifiable information duty”). The Regulations also provide for a system of penalties for failure to comply with that duty and procedures for reviewing and appealing penalties.

items.title: The Fostering Panels and Care Planning (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Care Planning, Placement and Case Review (Wales) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”) and the Fostering Panels (Establishment and Functions) (Wales) Regulations 2018 (“the 2018 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical Services Contracts) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical Services Contracts) (Wales) Regulations 2023 (“the 2023 Regulations”). Those regulations set out, for Wales, the framework for general medical services contracts under section 42 of the National Health Service (Wales) Act 2006.

items.title: The Beavers (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 9 to the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981 (“the 1981 Act”) and Schedule 2 to the Conservation of Habitats and Species Regulations 2017 (“the 2017 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education (Student Finance) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 2) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend various Regulations which make provision about, and in connection with, student finance.

items.title: The Regulated Services (Registration) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Regulated Services (Registration) (Wales) Regulations 2017 (“the principal Regulations”) make provision about the form and content of applications for registration and applications for variation of registration under Part 1 of the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (“the 2016 Act”).

items.title: The Building Act 1984 (Amendment of Commencement Order No. 1) and Building Safety Act 2022 (Amendment of Commencement Regulations No. 6) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations correct an error in the Welsh text of both the Building Act 1984 (Commencement No. 1) (Wales) Order 2025 and the Building Safety Act 2022 (Commencement No. 6) (Wales) Regulations 2025 to insert the coming into force date of 1 July 2026 into article 2 and regulation 2 respectively.

items.title: The Marine Licensing (Exempted Activities) (Wales) (Amendment) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends the Marine Licensing (Exempted Activities) (Wales) Order 2011 (“the 2011 Order”). The 2011 Order specifies licensable marine activities in respect of which a marine licence under Part 4 of the Marine and Coastal Access Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”) is not required, whether subject to conditions or otherwise.

items.title: The Care and Support (Charging) and (Financial Assessment) (Wales) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Care and Support (Charging) (Wales) Regulations 2015 (“the Charging Regulations”) and the Care and Support (Financial Assessment) (Wales) Regulations 2015 (“the Financial Assessment Regulations”).

items.title: The Code of Practice on Quality Assurance and Performance Management, Escalating Concerns and Closure of Regulated Care and Support Services (Appointed Day) (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order appoints 31 March 2026 as the day on which the Code of Practice on Quality Assurance and Performance Management, Escalating Concerns and Closure of Regulated Care and Support Services (“the Code of Practice”) comes into force.

items.title: The Recognition of Professional Qualifications and Implementation of International Recognition Agreements (Regulation and Inspection of Social Care) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations make provision under section 3(1) and (2) of the Professional Qualifications Act 2022 (c. 20) (“the 2022 Act”) to amend the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (anaw 2) (“the 2016 Act”) in relation to the implementation in relation to Wales of provisions relating to the recognition of professional qualifications contained in:

items.title: The Infrastructure Consent (Correcting Errors and Applications to Change or Revoke Infrastructure Consent Orders) (Procedure) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Infrastructure (Wales) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”) establishes a unified application and consenting process to enable making and consideration of applications for infrastructure consent. The process applies to the significant infrastructure projects that are specified in Part 1 of the 2024 Act. Broadly, they are energy, transport, waste and water projects.

items.title: The Tax Collection and Management (Visitor Levy Costs) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: Section 24A of the Tax Collection and Management (Wales) Act 2016 requires the Welsh Revenue Authority (“WRA”) to pay the proceeds of the visitor levy to the relevant principal council but only after WRA has deducted its costs and disbursements. Subsection (5) provides the Welsh Ministers with a regulation making power to make further provision about the deduction of amounts in respect of those costs and disbursements.

items.title: The Countryside and Rights of Way Act 2000 (Review of Maps) (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend section 10(2) of the Countryside and Rights of Way Act 2000 (c. 37) (“the Act”) in relation to Wales.

items.title: The Social Care Wales (Proceedings before Panels) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Care Wales (Proceedings before Panels) Regulations 2016 (“the 2016 Regulations”).

items.title: The School Teachers’ Pay and Conditions (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order makes provision for the determination of the remuneration of school teachers in Wales and other conditions of their employment which relate to their professional duties or working time.

items.title: The Education (Student Finance) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations, which are in eight Parts, amend various regulations which make provision about, and in connection with, student finance.

items.title: The Public Health (Minimum Price for Alcohol) (Wales) Act 2018 (Continuation) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Public Health (Minimum Price for Alcohol) (Wales) Act 2018 (“the 2018 Act”) makes provision about the minimum price for which alcohol is to be supplied by alcohol retailers from qualifying premises in Wales to a person in Wales and establishes a local authority led enforcement regime.

items.title: The Independent Review of Determinations (Adoption and Fostering) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations are made by the Welsh Ministers under powers conferred by the Adoption and Children Act 2002 and the Social Services and Well-being (Wales) Act 2014. They revoke and replace the Independent Review of Determinations (Adoption and Fostering) (Wales) Regulations 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”).

items.title: The Senedd and Elections (Wales) Act 2020 and the Senedd Cymru (Members and Elections) Act 2024 (Consequential Amendments) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations make minor amendments to secondary legislation, consequential on the renaming of the National Assembly for Wales to Senedd Cymru by section 2 of the Senedd and Elections (Wales) Act 2020 (“the 2020 Act”). Section 39 of the 2020 Act gives the Welsh Ministers power to make supplemental, incidental, saving or consequential provision if they consider it appropriate for the purposes of, in consequence of, or for giving effect to any provision of the 2020 Act. Section 150A of the Government of Wales Act 2006 operates in such a way as to change legally references to the new name of the institution. Leaving references to “the Assembly” or “the National Assembly for Wales” in the provisions being amended in these Regulations may however create confusion for the reader. These amendments are therefore considered appropriate for the purposes of clarity and accessibility of the legislation.

items.title: The Cancellation of Student Loans for Living Costs Liability (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations govern the student loan liability of full-time students who receive loans for living costs from the Welsh Ministers in respect of the academic year 2026/2027.

items.title: The Public Health (Minimum Price for Alcohol) (Minimum Unit Price) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Public Health (Minimum Price for Alcohol) (Wales) Act 2018 (“the 2018 Act”) makes provision about the minimum price for which alcohol is to be supplied by alcohol retailers from qualifying premises in Wales to a person in Wales and establishes a local authority led enforcement regime.

items.title: The Traffic Signs (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations and General Directions 2026
items.description: This Instrument amends the Traffic Signs Regulations 2016 and the Traffic Signs General Directions 2016, together titled the Traffic Signs Regulations and General Directions 2016 (“the 2016 Instrument”). The amendments apply only in relation to Wales.

items.title: The Childcare Act 2006 (Local Authority Assessment) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend requirements placed on local authorities under the Childcare Act 2006 (Local Authority Assessment) (Wales) Regulations 2016 (“the 2016 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services Contracts and Patient Charges) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations set out, for Wales, the framework for general dental services contracts under section 57 of the National Health Service Act 2006 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Regulated Services (Registration) (Wales) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Regulated Services (Registration) (Wales) Regulations 2017 (“the principal Regulations”) make provision about the form and content of applications for registration and applications for variation of registration under Part 1 of the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (“the 2016 Act”).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Value of Differential Multipliers) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: In relation to Wales, the non-domestic rating multiplier for each chargeable financial year is calculated in accordance with Part A2 of Schedule 7 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”).

items.title: The Local Health Boards (Directed Functions) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Health Boards (Directed Functions) (Wales) Regulations 2009 (S.I. 2009/1511 (W. 147)) (“the 2009 Regulations”).

items.title: The Land Transaction Tax (Modification of Relief for Acquisitions Involving Multiple Dwellings) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 13 to the Land Transaction Tax and Anti-avoidance of Devolved Taxes (Wales) Act 2017, which provides relief for acquisitions involving multiple dwellings.

items.title: The Renting Homes (Model Written Statements of Contract) (Wales) (Amendments etc.) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Renting Homes (Model Written Statements of Contract) (Wales) Regulations 2022 (S.I. 2022/28 (W. 13)) (“the 2022 Regulations”).

items.title: The Land Transaction Tax and Anti-avoidance of Devolved Taxes (Wales) Act 2017 (Amendments to Schedule 5) Regulations 2026
items.description: Schedule 5 to the Land Transaction Tax and Anti-avoidance of Devolved Taxes (Wales) Act 2017 (“the LTTA”) makes provision about transactions that are higher rates residential property transactions.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Artificial Avoidance Arrangements) (Local Lists) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: Sections 63F to 63M of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the Act”), which were inserted by section 13 of the Local Government Finance (Wales) Act 2024, make provision about counteracting advantages arising from artificial arrangements for the avoidance of non-domestic rates liability in relation to hereditaments in Wales.

items.title: The Closure of European Union Legacy Agriculture Schemes (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations close European Union Legacy Agriculture Schemes in Wales.

The Non-Party Campaigner Campaign Expenditure (Senedd Elections) Code of Practice 2025 (Appointed Day) (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order appoints 18 February 2026 as the day on which the Non-Party Campaigner Campaign Expenditure (Senedd Elections) Code of Practice 2025 (“Code of Practice”) comes into force.

items.title: The Political Parties Campaign Expenditure (Senedd Elections) Code of Practice 2025 (Appointed Day) (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order appoints 18 February 2026 as the day on which the Political Parties Campaign Expenditure (Senedd Elections) Code of Practice 2025 (“Code of Practice”) comes into force.

items.title: The Individual Candidate Election Expenses (Senedd Elections) Code of Practice 2025 (Appointed Day) (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order appoints 18 February 2026 as the day on which the Individual Candidate Election Expenses (Senedd Elections) Code of Practice 2025 (“Code of Practice”) comes into force.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services Contracts and Patient Charges) (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: The National Health Service (General Dental Services Contracts and Patient Charges) (Wales) Regulations 2026 (“the principal Regulations”) set out, for Wales, the framework for general dental services contracts under section 57 of the National Health Service (Wales) Act 2006.

items.title: The Environment (Air Quality and Soundscapes) (Wales) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2026
items.description: This Order is the third commencement order made by the Welsh Ministers under the Environment (Air Quality and Soundscapes) (Wales) Act 2024 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Education (Information About Individual Pupils and Children in Alternative Provision) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend—

items.title: The Education Workforce Council (Further Education Teacher Qualifications, Periods of Absence from Work and Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education Workforce Council (Main Functions) (Wales) Regulations 2015 (“the 2015 Regulations”) and the Education Workforce Council (Additional Categories of Registration and Further Education Teacher Qualifications) (Wales) Order 2024 (“the 2024 Order”).

items.title: The Higher Education (Qualifying Courses and Qualifying Persons) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: Mae’r Rheoliadau hyn yn pennu’r cyrsiau cymhwysol a’r personau cymhwysol at ddibenion adran 32 o Ddeddf Addysg Drydyddol ac Ymchwil (Cymru) 2022 (“Deddf 2022”).

items.title: The Regulated Adoption Services (Service Providers and Responsible Individuals) and Adoption Support Services (Local Authorities) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the Regulated Adoption Services (Service Providers and Responsible Individuals) (Wales) Regulations 2019 (“the 2019 Regulations”) and the Adoption Support Services (Local Authorities) (Wales) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”) under powers conferred by the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (“the 2016 Act”) and the Adoption and Children Act 2002 (“the 2002 Act”).

items.title: The Higher Education (Fee Limits) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations specify the maximum fee limits that apply for the purposes of section 46(6) of the Tertiary Education and Research (Wales) Act 2022 (“the 2022 Act”). These Regulations also provide that where fees are paid to a person other than a provider registered with the Commission for Tertiary Education and Research (“the Commission”), those fees are to be treated as being paid to the registered provider.

items.title: The Building Safety Act 2022 (Consequential Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to legislation which are consequential on Part 3 of the Building Safety Act 2022 (“the 2022 Act”).

items.title: The Food Supplements (Magnesium L-threonate monohydrate) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations are made using powers set out in regulations 2(2), 3 and 5(1) of the Nutrition (Amendment etc.) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019 (S.I. 2019/651) (“the 2019 Regulations”).

items.title: The Charges for Residues Surveillance (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Charges for Residues Surveillance Regulations 2006 (S.I. 2006/2285) in relation to Wales.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance and Accounting) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Authorities (Capital Finance and Accounting) (Wales) Regulations 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”).

items.title: The Independent Review of Determinations (Adoption and Fostering) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Independent Review of Determinations (Adoption and Fostering) (Wales) Regulations 2026 (“the 2026 Regulations”) to correct two cross-references to the Adoption Agencies (Wales) Regulations 2005 (“the Agencies Regulations”).

items.title: The Renters’ Rights Act 2025 (Commencement) (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order, made by the Welsh Ministers, brings into force the provisions of Chapter 4 of Part 1 of the Renters’ Rights Act 2025 (“the Act”). These provisions relate to the prohibition of discriminatory bans and restrictions in relation to a dwelling that is to be the subject of an occupation contract on the basis that a child would or may live with or visit a person at the dwelling, or on the basis that a person is or may be a benefits claimant.

items.title: The Building etc. (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building Regulations 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”) as they apply in relation to Wales, the Building (Registered Building Control Approvers etc.) (Wales) Regulations 2024 (“the RBCA Regulations”) and the Building (Restricted Activities and Functions) (Wales) Regulations 2024 (“the Restricted Activities and Functions Regulations”).

items.title: The Housing Renewal Grants (Amendment) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Renewal Grants Regulations 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”), which set out the means test for determining the amount of grant which may be paid by local housing authorities under Chapter 1 of Part 1 of the Housing Grants, Construction and Regeneration Act 1996. Certain payments which fall within the definition of “access funds” are disregarded for the purpose of the means test.

items.title: The Tertiary Education and Research (Wales) Act 2022 (Commencement No. 7, Transitory, Transitional and Savings Provisions) Order 2026
items.description: This Order brings into force provisions of the Tertiary Education and Research (Wales) Act 2022 (“the Act”) and makes transitory, transitional and savings provision in connection with the coming into force of certain provisions.

items.title: The National Health Service (Performers Lists) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the National Health Service (Performers Lists) (Wales) Regulations 2004 (S.I. 2004/1020 (W. 117)) (the “2004 Regulations”).

items.title: The Fire and Rescue Services (National Framework and Variation of Combination Schemes) (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: Section 21 of the Fire and Rescue Services Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”) requires the Welsh Ministers to prepare a Fire and Rescue National Framework. The framework must set out priorities and objectives for fire and rescue authorities in Wales and may provide guidance to those fire and rescue authorities in connection with the discharge of any of their functions and any other matter relating to those fire and rescue authorities or their functions that the Welsh Ministers consider appropriate. Fire and rescue authorities in Wales must have regard to the framework in carrying out their functions.

items.title: The Infrastructure Consent (Compensation for Changing or Revoking Infrastructure Consent Orders) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Infrastructure (Wales) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”) establishes a unified application and consenting process to enable making and consideration of applications for infrastructure consent. The process applies to the significant infrastructure projects that are specified in Part 1 of the 2024 Act. Broadly, they are energy, transport, waste and water projects.

items.title: The School Funding, Budget Statements and Outturn Statements (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Education (Budget Statements) (Wales) Regulations 2002, the Education (Outturn Statements) (Wales) Regulations 2003 and the School Funding (Wales) Regulations 2010 in relation to financial years that commence on or after 1 April 2027. These Regulations make provision for the funding of maintained schools in Wales for those financial years.

items.title: The Inspection of Education and Training (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Inspection of Education and Training (Wales) Regulations 2001, which were made under Part 4 of the Learning and Skills Act 2000.

items.title: The Infrastructure (Wales) Act 2024 (Amendment to Commencement Order No. 2) Order 2026
items.description: This Order makes amendments to the Infrastructure (Wales) Act 2024 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2025 (“the 2025 Order”) made by the Welsh Ministers under the Infrastructure (Wales) Act 2024.

items.title: The Online Safety Act 2023 (Exempt User-to-User and Search Services) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend Part 2 of Schedule 1 to the Online Safety Act 2023 (“the 2023 Act”), which is a list of descriptions of education and childcare services which are exempt from being regulated user-to-user or search services, as defined in section 4 of the 2023 Act (being exempt means that they are not subject to certain duties and to regulation by the Office of Communications).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Amendment of Definition of Domestic Property) (Wales) Order 2026
items.description: This Order amends section 66 of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the Act”) which defines domestic property for the purposes of Part 3 (non-domestic rating) of that Act.

items.title: The Infrastructure Consent (Fees) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: The Infrastructure (Wales) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”) establishes a unified application and consenting process to enable making and consideration of applications for infrastructure consent. The process applies to the significant infrastructure projects that are specified in Part 1 of the 2024 Act. Broadly, they are energy, transport, waste and water projects.

items.title: The Waste Separation Requirements (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Waste Separation Requirements (Wales) Regulations 2023 (“the 2023 Regulations”).

items.title: The Tertiary Education and Research (Wales) Act 2022 (Consequential Amendments and Transitory Provision) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 146 of the Tertiary Education and Research (Wales) Act 2022 (“the 2022 Act”) which enables the Welsh Ministers to make provision for the purposes of, in consequence of, or for giving full effect to any provision of the 2022 Act.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Vehicle Emissions) (Fixed Penalty) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Traffic (Vehicle Emissions) (Fixed Penalty) (Wales) Regulations 2003. They provide that an appropriate authority may issue fixed penalty notices in relation to stationary idling offences for amounts from within a range.

items.title: The Annual Returns (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Regulated Services (Annual Returns) (Wales) Regulations 2017 (“the 2017 Regulations”), which were made under section 10 of the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (“the 2016 Act”). They also make a minor amendment to the Regulated Services (Penalty Notices) (Wales) Regulations 2019, arising from changes to section 48 of the 2016 Act introduced by section 14(3)(c) of the Health and Social Care (Wales) Act 2025 (“the 2025 Act”).

items.title: The Social Partnership and Public Procurement (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations supplement the Social Partnership and Public Procurement (Wales) Act 2023 (“SPPPA 2023”).

items.title: The Senedd Cymru (Returning Officers’ Charges) Order 2026
items.description: This Order is made under article 23 of the Senedd Cymru (Representation of the People) Order 2025 (S.I. 2025/864) (W. 150) (“2025 Order”) which provides that a returning officer is entitled to recover charges in respect of services rendered or expenses incurred for, or in connection with, a Senedd Cymru election.

items.title: The Senedd Cymru (Returning Officers’ Accounts) Order 2026
items.description: This Order makes provision as to the time when, and the manner and form in which, accounts are to be submitted for the purpose of the payment of returning officers’ charges.

items.title: The Council Tax (Discounts, Disregards and Exemptions) (Wales) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations are made by the Welsh Ministers in exercise of their powers under the Local Government Finance Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”) and the Local Government Finance (Wales) Act 2024 (“the 2024 Act”).

items.title: The Welsh Elections Information Platform (Amendments) Regulations 2026
items.description: These Regulations amend the Welsh Elections Information Platform Regulations 2025 (“the 2025 Regulations”).
